title: ugputc
text: ugputc Copy data into an occurrence of the specified entity, which has been initialized by ugput . Explain this code short ugput (unsigned char*entity, unsigned char*buffer, shortlen): Copy Parameters entity The name of the entity you want to work on. A value of NULL or an empty string denotes the current entity. The entity must be painted on the component, and it must be defined in the application model. buffer The data to be used. This data must be in DBMS storage format. len The number of bytes to be copied from the buffer to the occurrence. Specify a negative value to indicate that more data is to follow. The absolute value of this number should not exceed the size of the buffer. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ugputc Value Meaning >1 The total number of bytes copied by this call and by previous calls to ugputc (returned when Len > 1) 1 Uniface expects more data to be copied (returned when Len < 0) <0 The occurrence was not initialized with a call to ugput , or the entity is not a painted database entity Use Library: yrtl Description Use ugputc to move data from buffer to an occurrence of entity that was initialized with ugput . If entity is NULL or an empty string, the current entity is used. The data in buffer should be in DBMS storage format. Do not supply primary key values that are different from the values that were passed to ugput . Uniface does not check on this, and will use the values passed to ugputc . Use a negative value for len to indicate that there is more data to be moved to the occurrence. Use a non-negative value to indicate that the current block is the last block of data. This makes it possible to fill a large occurrence using a small buffer by repeated calls to ugputc . The processing is not complete until you call ugputc one last time with a non-negative value for len . Once this is done, further calls to ugputc will return -1 until ugput is called again to initialize a new occurrence. Example: Calling ugputc Repeatedly The following simple example illustrates how to fill an occurrence of the current entity with the value ‘ abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz ’ by repeated calls to ugputc with a length of -1. Note the concluding call to ugputc with a zero length; without that call, the data will not be shown in the component. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void fill_occ () { int i; ugput(0, 0, 0); /* Initialize the occurrence */ for ( i=0; i<26; i++ ) { char ch = 'a'+i; gputc(0, &ch, -1); /* Add one alphabet character */ } ugputc(0, 0, 0); /* Terminate processing */ } Copy Related Links ugput Initialize an occurrence of the specified entity for subsequent calls to ugputc.
